title: Hieracium transbalticum
intro: Hieracium transbalticum, commonly known as Trans-Baltic Hawkweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the dandelion-like genus Hieracium. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of wounds and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium transbalticum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Trans-Baltic Hawkweed include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to support skin health and to treat minor wounds.
taxonomy: Hieracium transbalticum belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This plant is classified within the genus Hieracium, which includes approximately 1000 species of hawkweeds. As a member of the genus Hieracium, Hieracium transbalticum is further classified into various species and subspecies based on its morphological and genetic characteristics. The exact classification of Hieracium transbalticum may vary depending on the source and the specific taxonomic system used. Hieracium transbalticum is typically classified as a perennial herbaceous plant.
